item.title: Watch Live: Hegseth, Caine testify before Senate panel amid escalating Iran war
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the administration estimates that the Iran war has cost $37.5 billion since it began in late February.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens, threatening Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Universities get funding from foreign entities on U.S. watch lists, records show
item.description: The State Department is cracking down on universities accepting funding from foreign entities on U S. government watch lists.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine's top general after days of protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues.

item.title: Watch: Sen. Kennedy presses Caine and Hegseth on Ukraine, Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Sen. Ted Kennedy on Tuesday pressed Gen. Dan Caine and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth on Russia, Ukraine and what would happen to the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. ended its war with Iran.

item.title: Watch: Hegseth, Sen. Murray clash over Iran war funding request
item.description: Sen. Patty Murray on Tuesday pressed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth over what she views as an "attempt to skirt the normal appropriations process," accusing the Trump administration of poor planning as it requests an additional $67 billion in funding as the Iran war escalates.

item.title: Watch: Hegseth defends $67 billion request to help cover cost of Iran war
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth appeared before a Senate committee on Tuesday to defend the Trump administration's request for $67 billion in emergency funding as the number of U.S. service members killed in the Iran conflict rises to at least 17.

item.title: U.S. universities received millions from foreign entities on U.S. watch lists, records show
item.description: Major U.S. universities have received millions of dollars in funding from foreign entities that are on U.S. government watch lists, according to a CBS News review of university funding records. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Friends remember 19-year-old U.S. soldier Isabella Gonzales after death in Iran strike
item.description: Isabella Gonzales' friends in Texas are fondly remembering the fallen soldier as they come to terms with her death on Friday in the U.S. war with Iran. CBS News Texas reports.

item.title: Iran attacks U.S. allies as 3 American soldiers killed in escalating war
item.description: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as at least 3 U.S. forces are killed and dozens wounded in the escalating conflict.

item.title: Ex-U.S. attorney in Seattle sues Trump over firing
item.description: Roger Rogoff was fired from his role as U.S. attorney in Seattle shortly after judges on the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington appointed him to the job.

item.title: Inside the use of 70mm IMAX cameras on "The Odyssey"
item.description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" has made history as the first feature-length film shot entirely on 70mm IMAX cameras. Jay Holben, technical editor for the American Cinematographer, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Trump to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian hockey sticks, alcohol, other goods
item.description: President Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, from hockey equipment to alcoholic beverages, escalating the trade dispute between the two countries.

item.title: More Americans report shoplifting as inflation climbs, survey finds
item.description: Thirty percent of respondents said they shoplifted this year, with most citing affordability concerns and financial hardship.

item.title: Software error improperly registered 6,600 noncitizens to vote, N.J. gov. says
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a preliminary analysis showed that fewer than 400 of the people registered that way went on to cast ballots.

item.title: Trump says Lebanon has been "badly treated" as Aoun pushes for withdrawal of Israeli troops
item.description: President Trump conceded that Lebanon has been "badly treated" following a meeting with President Joseph Aoun on Tuesday, pledging to help the country be "properly treated" as the Lebanese reprieve from the presence of Israeli forces in their southern territory. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Markets undeterred so far as Trump imposes additional 50% tariffs on some Canadian goods
item.description: President Trump plans to impose a new 50% tariff on certain Canadian goods in addition to his existing tariffs. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady explains the tariffs and investor reaction.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $50,000 2-year CD account can earn now
item.description: Have $50,000 you're considering moving into a 2-year CD account? Here's how much interest you'd earn if you do.

item.title: Kaylee Hottle, actress in "Godzilla vs. Kong," dies at 18 in car crash
item.description: Kaylee Hottle, an Atlanta-born actress known for her role in the "Godzilla" film franchise, has died following a crash in Maryland.

item.title: Timeline of explosion outside New York City federal building
item.description: A veteran was arrested for allegedly setting off fireworks and starting a fire in front of a Manhattan building that houses several federal offices, including ICE, the FBI says. CBS News' Anna Schecter shares more details.

item.title: Taco Bell reports drop in foot traffic amid cyclosporiasis outbreak, probe continues into source
item.description: The FDA continues to hunt for the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak, though it says its investigation "continue to converge on shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms locations in central Mexico." Fast food giant Taco Bell, which uses shredded lettuce in some products, has seen declining foot traffic as some Americans avoid raw produce. CBS News' Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Here's how much wealth you need to thrive, based on your age
item.description: Three in four American households lack the wealth to weather financial setbacks and achieve major life milestones.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, accusing it of false advertising
item.description: The lawsuit alleges that Eli Lilly misled consumers by using outdated clinical trial data to make its drugs appear more effective than Novo's.

item.title: FDA still probing Taylor Farms lettuce despite false positive test
item.description: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing" in the cyclosporiasis outbreak.

item.title: What to know about Taylor Farms amid the cyclospora outbreak
item.description: The nation's largest recent cyclospora outbreak has put one of America's biggest produce suppliers under renewed scrutiny.

item.title: Zelenskyy's move to oust popular defense chief draws major backlash
item.description: Ukrainians are demonstrating in Kyiv and senior figures announcing their resignations over President Volodymyr Zelenksyy's move to oust his popular defense chief.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Can You Hack Your Brain to Stop Impulse Buying? | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
"Dopamine sites" are on the rise in South Korea, allowing people to simulate the thrill of online shopping or food delivery without spending any actual money. Could this be the fix for compulsive spenders? Plus, Jill helps caller Danielle navigate a financial crossroads: after taking a pay cut to make work and life more manageable, she's now facing higher-than-expected college costs for her child while trying to stay on track for retirement.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: What to know about "explosive" diarrhea-causing parasite reported in several states
item.description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection, called cyclosporiasis, that causes severe diarrhea.
